programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: The story of Los Angeles' most infamous family-run mortuary, when David Sconce took over Lamb Funeral Home in the early 1980s and engaged in morally questionable and inhumane practices to increase his earnings.

programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: When the Lamb Funeral Home's crematorium is destroyed by a fire in 1986, an undeterred David Sconce finds ways to expand the family business. As allegations of abuse mount, he is eventually arrested and the ensuing police investigation uncovers shocking evidence that points to Sconce being possibly connected to the assault and later death of a rival mortician.

programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: After members of the Lamb family are convicted of fraud and various other crimes, a murder charge is brought against David Sconce for the death of his rival, but the case is dropped due to a lack of hard evidence. With more than 20,000 families affected by the practices at the Lamb Funeral Home, the case brings about reforms and new regulations to the mortuary business.

programme.title: Outcry
programme.desc: After two years of legal limbo, Kelley's fate is finally determined as more details of a possible conspiracy to deny him of his innocence emerge.

programme.title: Whale with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve freedives with whales and dolphins, getting closer than ever before to record rarely seen behaviours from these extraordinary mammals. He also teams up with world-leading scientists to confront the uncomfortable and urgent truths of overfishing and pollution, along the way uncovering how whales and dolphins are adapting to changing oceans in unexpected ways.

programme.title: Building Outside the Lines

programme.title: Restoring Galveston

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: To balance design, family and functionality, Michael and Ashley build a staircase with custom newel post, add a fireplace with an old-wood mantel, build legs for their 12-foot island and put 5-foot cast iron kitchen sinks in their primary bathroom.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordray family has been in Galveston for over a century, and for 50 years they ran a corner drug store; Ashley wants to bring it back; with a baby due in a few weeks, Michael doesn't think it's a good time to open a new business.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley turn a dilapidated wood mill from 1908 into a wedding venue complete with limestone-like stamped concrete, a wedding arbor, lathe table bar, a grand staircase and two dressing suites.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The team honors grandfather Bampy Hersom's memory by continuing renovations on his cabin and creating a bigger bathroom and kitchen.
